4 min read
Every ESP is going to have a different way of identifying
unengaged leads. If you use ConvertKit, and you’re on their
Creator Pro plan, you’ll have access to their ‘subscriber scoring’
feature, which is what we use.
A word of warming before we go on. Many ESPs, ConvertKit
included, have the ability to automatically DELETE a subscriber. Be
very (very!) careful using any delete feature within an automated
process, for obvious reasons.
We prefer tagging people “for deletion,” which we’ll then execute
manually once we know the extent of the purge.
One of the signals ConvertKit keeps track of, is an Engagement
Score across �ve scoring levels — 5 Stars down to 1 Star.
▪ 1 Star = subscribers who have not engaged with your content
in the past 9 months.
▪ 2 Star = subscribers who have engaged with your content in
the past 9 months.
▪ 3 Star = subscribers who have engaged with your content in
the past 6 months.
Re-Engagement Series
www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 10 --

▪ 4 Star = subscribers who have engaged with your content in
the past 90 days.
▪ 5 Star — subscribers who have engaged with your content in
the past 30 days, or purchased one of your products.
(The large majority of our subscribers are categorized as 5 Star.)
Below is a segment we’ve created to group “1 Star” subscribers. In
the example below, we have 24 subscribers matching this criteria.
We’ll then (manually) trigger a ‘Bulk Action’ to add all these
www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 10 --

subscribers to our Reengagement Series (click the segment name,
select all subscribers within the 1 Star segment, Bulk Actions Add
Tag, then select the tag you’ll use to trigger your automation, as
seen below).
www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 10 --

www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 10 --

As mentioned, this automation will not automatically delete
subscribers who don’t re-engage. It’ll set a custom �eld that’ll
identify whether they’re “reactivated” or marked “for deletion.”
If someone re-engages by clicking any of the re-engagement link
triggers within any of the emails, they’ll be marked as re-engaged
and pulled out of the sequence and exit the automation.
Here’s our three-email series.
Subject: ACTION REQUIRED: Is this a
goodbye?
Not to be too dramatic, but it turns out you have been �agged as
“inactive,” (shocker!) a fancy way of saying you’ve not clicked a link
within any emails from us in the past three months.
We’re sensitive souls…
… so, learning that you’ve not been engaging with our emails saddens
us.
It feels like we’ve let you down somehow, didn’t ful�ll our promise to
you of delivering relevant and meaningful content.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 10 --

(Apologies if this is in error — tracking “opens” is horribly inaccurate,
so even if you’re reading every one of our emails but haven’t clicked
any link, you can still be �agged as “inactive.”)
If you want to continue to receive emails from us, your ACTION IS
REQUIRED.
Click this link if you want to remain on our email list.
Otherwise, do nothing, and you’ll be automatically removed in 72
hours.
Whichever choice you make, we get it.
We’re all busy as hell.
Life gets in the way.
Sh%t happens.
So if you do nothing, you’ll be REMOVED from our email list in three
days.
But if you want to continue to receive best-in-class counter-intuitive
insights, ideas, and lessons about modern marketing…
… then, we need you to indicate your intent by clicking here.
Either way, it has been an honor and privilege to serve you.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 10 --

Wishing you all the very best…
Andre & Shawn
Subject: Houston, we have a problem!
We sent you an email yesterday to let you know that you’ve been
�agged as “inactive” by our email service provider…
… and, as a result, we’ll be removing you from our email system in
two days.
Unless you want to stay…
If you do nothing, your email address will be removed automatically
and you won’t receive emails from us again unless you re-subscribe.
We’re all about relevancy…
Maybe your circumstances have changed since adding yourself to our
email list (or purchasing a product from us)…
…or you’re just getting too many emails.
We get it, protecting our attention and inbox is critical.
If, however, you do want to remain on our list, click here and you’ll be
all set.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 10 --

(You may even hear Beethoven’s Ode to Joy while angels sing.)
We appreciate your attention and feel honored you chose to be on this
journey with us.
Andre & Shawn
P.S.
A few people have emailed saying they ARE opening our emails.
Here’s the thing, though…
Our email service provider uses image pixels to measure engagement.
But, many people either prevent images from loading (problem #1) or
have an ad blocker installed (problem #2).
What’s more, Apple, in an e�ort to further enhance privacy (with the
release of iOS 15 and macOS Monterey), will prevent newsletter
providers from tracking open rates. (This news piece explains more.)
Solution: Clicking links in our email will always indicate your
engagement.
If you’ve received this noti�cation and you’ve been opening our
emails, the best thing to do is to click the links in some of our
emails occasionally when it makes sense to you.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 10 --

That way, even if the image pixel doesn’t load, you’ll still be recorded
as having “clicked” (opened/read) the email.
We know this may be a pain in the butt, and we appreciate your help
keeping our email list “clean” and relevant to everyone on it.
Subject: We’re sorry to see you go.
We’re sorry to see you go.
You’ve not clicked a link inside any email in the past 90 days…
Which is a signal to us we’ve become irrelevant to you.
We’re all about relevancy…
If you do want to stay, click here.
Otherwise, no action is required by you. Your email will automatically
be deleted in about 24 hours.
Andre & Shawn
We’ll manually trigger and run this campaign every few months,
or whenever our ‘1 Star’ segment gets big enough.
Please ask questions and leave comments below.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 10 --

—André & Shawn
www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 10 --
